name_cn: Heavy Load at Feeder End Nodes
name_en: Heavy Load at Feeder End Nodes
description_cn: Simulates low voltage, increased line power flow, and higher grid losses caused by heavy loads at feeder-end nodes.
description_en: Simulates voltage and loss stress caused by heavy load at feeder end nodes.
typical_user_requests: Apply heavy load at feeder-end nodes
Simulate low voltage at feeder ends

global_ev_multiplier.description: Do not introduce EV charging demand unless explicitly requested.

validation_rules.message_cn: Neither global load nor node load was increased, so the feeder-end heavy-load characteristics may not be visible.
The global load is reduced without adding node load, which conflicts with the heavy-load scenario semantics.
The added load does not target recommended feeder-end nodes and may weaken the intended low-voltage stress.
Added load at a single node exceeds 1500 kW. Confirm that this matches the engineering setup.
Added load at a single node exceeds 2000 kW and is outside the current safety boundary.

validation_rules.condition: effective_load_multiplier >= 2.0 and use_ess == false and use_sop == false
validation_rules.message_cn: ESS and SOP are both disabled under high load stress, leaving insufficient voltage-support resources and increasing operational risk.
Concentrated EV charging combined with feeder-end heavy load may further increase low-voltage and line-flow risk.

result_interpretation_focus: Focus on whether the minimum feeder-end voltage improves.
Higher grid loss indicates line-flow stress caused by heavy loading.
Lower maximum line power flow indicates that the control strategy relieves local flow stress.
